title: Leaf-Wrap-Steam American Grape Juice Tagine
description: A comforting American dish featuring a rich grape juice flavor with crunchy leaf-wrapped steam.

ingredients.name: Leaves (such as lettuce or collard greens)

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together grape juice, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the wheat flour and mix until the dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of butter to the skillet and let it melt.
Place 1-2 leaves in the skillet, depending on their size.
Place a dough disk in the center of each leaf.
Add 1-2 cloves of garlic on top of each dough disk.
Drizzle a little bit of grape juice over the garlic.
Fold the leaves over the dough to enclose it, pressing the edges together to seal the tagine.
Cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until the crust is golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining dough and leaves.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh thyme and rosemary.
Serve immediately, or let it cool and refrigerate for up to 3 days.
Reheat the tagine by steaming the leaves for 2-3 minutes before serving.
Serve with additional grape juice on the side, if desired.
Serve with a side salad or roasted vegetables for a complete meal.
